title: Tensor Food Figures
subtitle: Interactive browser view of the figures and figure groups used in the paper.

sections.title: Methods
sections.description: Figures referenced in the methods section.

figures.title: Horseshoe Theory of Salad
figures.caption: Horseshoe theory of salad: percent lettuce vs. percent croutons. The extremes (0,100) and (100,0) are salad (single component); the middle (50,50) is nachos. Dotted line: lettuce-crouton proportion gradient.

sections.title: Experiments
sections.description: Interactive versions of the experiment figures, including all tensor stages already exported by the plotting scripts.

figures.title: Pairwise Projections of T
figures.caption: Pairwise projections of T: slices over (i,j), (i,k), and (j,k). Black squares mark canonical occupied cells, gray squares mark accepted real LLM-identified cells, and white squares with black outlines mark LLM speculative cells; empty cells are not shown. Omitted categories by projection for the speculated stage: Cube vs. Protein (Cube: none; Protein: none); Cube vs. Starch (Cube: none; Starch: Other Grain); Protein vs. Starch (Protein: none; Starch: Other Grain).

figures.title: 3D Occupancy Tensor
figures.caption: Interactive 3D occupancy tensor view. Black markers indicate canonical occupied cells, gray markers indicate accepted real LLM-identified cells, and white markers with black outlines indicate LLM speculative cells; empty cells are not shown.

figures.title: LLM Confidence Histogram
figures.caption: Distribution of model confidence for accepted LLM-generated candidates. The shape is visibly non-parametric because these confidence values are bounded self-reports on [0,1], then truncated by the acceptance cutoff at 0.75, so the observed mass piles up at a few high-confidence values rather than following a Gaussian law.
